description: The attach/bind retry classification (PERSISTENCE_BIND_CONTRACT §5.3; extracted from the Ruby reference implementation, computer.rb). Three classes: readiness (deadline-bound convergence on a fresh pod), race (attempt-bound re-mint after a pod-identity shift), terminal (typed error, no same-pod retry).

policy.envelope: REUSE the held envelope byte-for-byte (same jti + ciphertext) — a settled bind answers consistently; the per-pod bind guard serializes

http_plain.note: ingress: no Ready pod backs this host yet
ingress: backend not up. ONLY a plain 404/503 (no problem_type) is a readiness signal — a problem-typed 404/503 is a real answer

policy.envelope: RE-MINT from scratch: refetch bind-pubkey, mint fresh attach credentials, reseal (the held envelope targets the old pod identity)

race_retry.notes: ephem-pub-mismatch is defensive — coord does not currently emit it; an ephem-key mismatch surfaces as hpke-decrypt-failed (terminal).

conditional.when: status >= 500 AND the message does NOT indicate an epoch conflict
conditional.note: transient broker/restore pipeline failure; the epoch-conflict variant is terminal (another pod won the lease)

terminal.note: single-use pod bound elsewhere — recycle, never same-pod replay
another pod holds this Computer's epoch lease

recovery_invariants: coord 401 on a previously-bound ct_ => RebindRequired; NEVER an implicit in-place rebind
ct_ renews by activity (sliding idle TTL); there is no refresh endpoint
